Place of birth.verification_summary: The place of birth must include the town or city and the country.

Nationality.verification_summary: The nationality must be written exactly as it appears in the official travel document.

Address.problem_in_given_value: The address is incomplete and contains invalid characters.
Address.verification_summary: The address must be written exactly as it appears in the official identity document.

Telephone.verification_summary: The telephone number must include the full international dialling code.

Email.verification_summary: The email address is optional, but if provided, it must be actively monitored.

Sex.verification_summary: The sex must be ticked as male or female.

Name.problem_in_given_value: The name of the organisation is missing.
Name.verification_summary: The name of the organisation must be written exactly as it appears in the official register.

Identification number.problem_in_given_value: The identification number of the organisation is missing.
Identification number.verification_summary: The identification number of the organisation must be provided if available.

Date of registration.problem_in_given_value: The date of registration of the organisation is missing.
Date of registration.verification_summary: The date of registration of the organisation must be written in the format DD/MM/YYYY if available.

Activity.problem_in_given_value: The activity of the organisation is missing.
Activity.verification_summary: The activity of the organisation must be described briefly.

Registered address.problem_in_given_value: The registered address of the organisation is missing.
Registered address.verification_summary: The registered address of the organisation must be written exactly as it appears in the official register.

Telephone.problem_in_given_value: The telephone number of the organisation is missing.
Telephone.verification_summary: The telephone number of the organisation must include the full international dialling code.

Email.problem_in_given_value: The email address of the organisation is missing.
Email.verification_summary: The email address of the organisation must be provided if available.

Selected.problem_in_given_value: The given value is incomplete. It should be a list of all states involved in the case.
Selected.verification_summary: The given value does not match the available states.

Capacity / relationship.verification_summary: The legal basis on which a person acts on the applicant’s behalf must be clearly established.

Surname.verification_summary: Write the representative’s family name as in their official identity document.

First name(s).verification_summary: Write all first names as in the official identity document. Do not abbreviate.

Nationality.verification_summary: Write the representative’s current nationality as in their official identity document.

Address.verification_summary: Provide a full postal address for correspondence: street, city, postcode and country.

Telephone.verification_summary: Include the full international dialling code. Indicate whether the number is mobile or landline.

Fax.verification_summary: Provide the fax number with full international dialling code, if available.

Surname.verification_summary: Write the lawyer’s family name as in professional records or the bar register.

First name(s).verification_summary: Write all first names as in the lawyer’s professional registration. Do not abbreviate.

Nationality.verification_summary: State the lawyer’s current nationality as in their official documentation.

Address.verification_summary: Provide the law firm’s or chambers’ professional postal address: street, city, postcode and country.

Telephone.verification_summary: Include the full international dialling code. Provide the lawyer’s direct professional number.

Fax.verification_summary: Provide a professional fax number with full international dialling code, if available.

Email.verification_summary: Provide a professional email address. This should match or be consistent with the eComms email at Field 37.

eComms email.given_value: address) alexandrepataraia@ yahoo.com By completing this field you agree to using the eComms system. European Court of Human Rights - Application form 4/13
eComms email.verification_summary: Use a generic institutional email address (e.g. echr@lawfirm.com) so the account can be accessed by multiple colleagues within the firm.

Capacity / function.verification_summary: An organisation can only act through a person legally authorised to represent it. The Court must verify this chain of authority. Without documentary proof, the application may be rejected for lack of proper authorisation.

Surname.verification_summary: The full name of the authorised official is required for the Court’s records and so that the official can be identified as the person who signs the authority in Field 53.

First name(s).verification_summary: All given names are required for unambiguous identification of the authorised official.

Nationality.verification_summary: The official’s nationality is part of their formal identification required under Rule 47.

Address.verification_summary: The official’s address enables the Court to correspond with the organisation’s authorised representative directly. If the organisation appoints a lawyer, correspondence will go to the lawyer; otherwise, it goes to the official at this address.

Telephone.verification_summary: Telephone contact for the official enables urgent Registry communications to reach the organisation.

Fax.verification_summary: An optional contact detail. A fax number cannot be used to lodge the application itself.

Email.verification_summary: Email contact for the organisation official provides an additional channel. The eComms system (Field 57) will be the main electronic communication channel for the lawyer once the application is notified.

Surname.verification_summary: Full identification of the lawyer representing the organisation is required under Rule 47. Once listed and signed, the lawyer becomes the Court’s primary correspondent.

First name(s).verification_summary: All given names are required for unambiguous identification in the Court’s records.

Nationality.verification_summary: Nationality is part of the formal identification of the lawyer required under Rule 47.

Address.verification_summary: The lawyer’s professional address becomes the primary correspondence address for the organisation’s application. All Registry communications will be directed here.

Telephone.verification_summary: A working telephone number for the lawyer allows the Registry to make urgent contact.

Fax.verification_summary: Optional. A fax number is only for subsequent correspondence; it cannot be used to lodge the application.

Email.verification_summary: The lawyer’s email at Field 52 is linked to the eComms account at Field 57. This becomes the primary electronic communication channel once the application is notified to the Government.

E. Statement of facts.given_value: 58. 1. The latter application concerns the possible violations of the European Convention on Human Rights (‘ECHR/ Convention’) in regards to the Applicant's ('A.D.') husband's ('N.K.') arrest and the inability due to the Georgian legislation to appeal the legality of the arrest in the Courts, the lack of fair trial and the subsequent murder of N.K. during him being placed in custody of the law enforcement officers and the ineffectiveness of the investigation of his murder. Thus, the Applicant alleges the violations of both the negative and positive aspects of Article 2, Article 5, Article 6(1)(2) and Article 13. 2. On February 15, 2020, the applicant's husband - N.K., a well-known businessman, was arrested, as there was a suspicion that the latter was allegedly providing assistance to a terrorist organization operating in the occupied Tskhinvali region/the so-called South Ossetia (Annex (‘A’) 1). In 2018-2019, the mentioned organization allegedly carried out several terrorist attacks, including in the center of Tbilisi, which caused the deaths of civilians, as well as 35 police officers (A2). 3. On February 20, 2020, N.K. was charged with the offense of Article 323' of the Criminal Code of Georgia ("Illegal purchase, storage, carrying, manufacturing, transportation, sending, taking or use of firearms, ammunition, explosive substances or explosive devices for terrorist purposes for the purpose") under Section 5 ("the action provided for in Sections 1, 2, 3 or 4 of this Article, which caused the death of a person or other serious consequences") (A3; A15). 4. On the same day, the Tbilisi City Court used detention as a preventive measure (A4). The court shared the position of the prosecution that "the applicant, as a well-known businessman with ties to a terrorist organization operating outside the territory controlled by Georgia, had the opportunity to hide, obstruct the administration of justice and influence witnesses" (A4). On April 21, 2020, N.K. appealed against the decision of the Tbilisi City Court (AS), however, the Court of Appeals declared the appeal inadmissible on the basis of failure to meet procedural requirements (A6). 5. Considering the circumstances of the case and the personality of the Applicant’s husband, the case under consideration was actively covered by the media (A7). During the arrest of the Applicant’s husband, one of the ministers made a public statement (A8). In the mentioned statement, the minister, among other things, said that the law-enforcement agencies arrested one of the most dangerous criminals who provided assistance to the terrorist organization (A8). The said statement was widely disseminated by the media (A8.1). 6. The Tbilisi City Court found the applicant's husband guilty and sentenced him to 15 years of imprisonment (A9). The Court of Appeals shared the decision of the City Court (A10). The national courts considered that since the prosecution had limited access to the occupied territories, the indirect testimony of thirteen employees of the Applicant’s husband constituted sufficient evidence to prove the applicant's guilt (A9; A10). On February 1, 2022, the Supreme Court declared NK's application inadmissible (A11). 7. On February 2, 2022, during the transfer to the penitentiary institution, N.K. was murdered as a result of a bullet fired by an unidentified person (A12.1; A12.2.2). The fact of NK being transferred to a penitentiary institution was actively covered by the media before the aforementioned murder (A12.2.1). 8. On February 2, 2022, the General Prosecutor's Office of Georgia initiated a criminal investigation regarding this murder of the Applicant's husband (A12.1). The Applicant - A.D. was actively involved in the investigation (A13). However, the status of the victim has not been officially assigned, and until now, criminal prosecution against a specific person has not been initiated (A14).

Article 61.given_value: Convention. Explanation 1. Article 2 ranks as one of the most fundamental provisions in the Convention, one which in peace time, admits of no derogation under Article 15. Together with Article 3, it enshrines one of the basic values of the democratic societies making up the Council of Europe (Giuliani and Gaggio v. Italy [GC], no. 23458/02, 24.03.2011, § 174.). As such, its provisions must be strictly construed (McCann and Others v. the United Kingdom, no. 18984/91, 27.09.1995, § 147). Article 2 contains two substantive obligations: the
Article 61.problem_in_given_value: The given value is too long and does not follow the required format. It should be a brief explanation of how the Article was violated, connected to the facts in Part E.
Article 61.verification_summary: The given value does not meet the verification guidelines. It should be a concise and legible statement of the alleged violation(s) and the relevant arguments.

Article 62.given_value: the Convention Explanation 5. Article 2 which imposes a duty on that State to secure the right to life by putting in place effective criminal-law provisions to deter the commission of offences against the person, backed up by law-enforcement machinery for the prevention, suppression and punishment of breaches of such provisions. This obligation requires by implication that there should be some form of effective official investigation when there is reason to believe that an individual has sustained life-threatening injuries in suspicious
Article 62.problem_in_given_value: The given value is too long and does not follow the required format. It should be a brief explanation of how the Article was violated, connected to the facts in Part E.
Article 62.verification_summary: The given value does not meet the verification guidelines. It should be a concise and legible statement of the alleged violation(s) and the relevant arguments.

Complaints summary.problem_in_given_value: The field is missing, which is not allowed.
Complaints summary.verification_summary: The applicant must provide a summary of the complaints.

Unused remedies.verification_summary: The applicant must answer Yes or No to indicate if there are unused remedies.

Why unused.problem_in_given_value: The field is missing, which is not allowed.
Why unused.verification_summary: The applicant must explain why unused remedies were not exhausted.

Any proceedings.problem_in_given_value: The field is missing and should be answered honestly.
Any proceedings.verification_summary: The Court examines this of its own motion. Deliberately concealing parallel proceedings constitutes an abuse of the right of application.

Details.problem_in_given_value: The field is missing and cannot be inferred.
Details.verification_summary: Qualifying bodies include: UN human rights treaty bodies (Human Rights Committee, CEDAW Committee, ILO Committee, etc.) and international arbitration panels. The Court considers the body’s legal character, competence and founding instrument.

Other ECHR applications.problem_in_given_value: The field is missing and should be answered honestly.
Other ECHR applications.verification_summary: Previous applications must be disclosed because they may be substantially the same as the current application (leading to inadmissibility), and because the Court needs to manage related cases efficiently and may link them for examination.

Application numbers.problem_in_given_value: The field is missing and cannot be inferred.
Application numbers.verification_summary: Application numbers allow the Court to trace previous files and assess whether the current application is genuinely new or a re-lodging of an already examined matter.

I. List of accompanying documents.problem_in_given_value: The list of accompanying documents is empty. According to the admissibility guide, all documents attached to the application must be listed.
I. List of accompanying documents.verification_summary: The application form requires a numbered, chronological list of all documents attached to the application.

Additional comments.problem_in_given_value: Missing value, cannot infer from context
Additional comments.verification_summary: Field 71 is not a substitute for the substantive sections E, F or G. The Court will not accept comments here as a replacement for facts, violations or remedies information that should appear in their respective sections.

Date.problem_in_given_value: Missing value, cannot infer from context
Date.verification_summary: The date of the declaration confirms when the form was finalised. It should correspond to the date of dispatch. The four-month deadline is calculated from the postmark, but significant discrepancies between the signature date and the dispatch date may prompt Registry queries.

Signed by.problem_in_given_value: Missing value, cannot infer from context
Signed by.verification_summary: The declaration is a mandatory formal requirement under Rule 47. The form is a legal document with legal consequences. Without an original signature, the application is not valid and will not be registered.

Correspondence contact.problem_in_given_value: Missing value, cannot infer from context
Correspondence contact.verification_summary: The Court corresponds with only one applicant or one representative. This ensures communications are not duplicated, lost or contradicted. The correspondent identified in Field 74 becomes the Court’s single point of contact for all subsequent correspondence.
